fields.summary: Allow auxiliary services to listen for container starts and completions

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: Making container start and completion events available to auxiliary services would allow them to be resource-aware.  The auxiliary service would be able to notify a co-located service that is opportunistically using free capacity of allocation changes.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Isolation of failures in aux services

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.summary: Refactor AuxServicesEvent into a AuxServicesAppEvent and AuxServicesContainerEvent

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.summary: JobHistoryServer has no information about applications if the MR-AM crashes

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

Can we add in the initializeContainer doc exactly when this will be called?  I.e. whether it's after getting a container launch command from the AM or whether it's after launching the actual container.

False whitespace change in ApplicationImpl.java

Otherwise, LGTM

Cancelling for addressing the minor comments.

Also,
 - AuxiliaryService.java: "An container is finishing" -> "A container is finishing"
 - Split AuxServicesEvent into a AuxServicesAppEvent and AuxServicesContainerEvent ? Don't like nulls like that.
 - I think the events are better raised in the Container state machine.

Thanks Sandy & Vinod. In the latest patch I've took care of all the changes except the following.

bq. Split AuxServicesEvent into a AuxServicesAppEvent and AuxServicesContainerEvent ? Don't like nulls like that.

The patch is only adding a new property to the event, container, which is NULL for App events. All the other NULLs where already there.

Regardless, I've tried refactoring AuxServicesEvent into a AuxServicesAppEvent and AuxServicesContainerEvent. But the patch gets much bigger as the necessary changes are not  just different names but the way the AuxiliaryServices handle() would take care of these 2 events. We should introduce a parent event class for those.

I'd prefer, if you still want to do this break up, to do it as part of another JIRA which only does the refactoring, without adding new functionality.
